purpose: Art-direction reference for the fullscreen five-plane V7 worldscape; not loaded directly by the runtime.
prompt: Use case: production art-direction concept for an original side-view pixel-art sandbox game's parallax worldscape. Create a wide 16:9 environment concept, no UI and no text. Show one cohesive lush fantasy forest at twilight filling the entire canvas edge-to-edge with no blank areas, hard horizontal cutoffs, flat gradient bands, or vignette. Clearly readable depth through at least five overlapping planes: atmospheric sky with small clouds and stars; very distant randomized mountain chains; sparse far floating islands with hanging roots and tiny waterfalls; middle-distance forests, ruins and mist pockets; richly silhouetted near trees, rocks and foliage. Strong Terraria-like readability and density as a genre reference but fully original shapes, palette and composition. Chunky high-detail pixel art, deliberate clusters, full canopies without random holes, nuanced teal/blue distance palette warming into moss green and amber near the camera. Design all planes to plausibly repeat horizontally while avoiding obvious mirrored symmetry, and leave a calm readable play-space band near the center/lower third. Crisp nearest-neighbor pixel aesthetic, no painterly blur, no 3D, no photorealism, no labels, no logo, no watermark.
